functions.name: stsynphot.commissioning.utils.count_outliers
documentation:
Count outliers in given data.

This is as defined in similar method in `SpecCase`
in `astrolib/pysynphot/from_commissioning/conv_base.py`.

:::{note}
This is not used but kept for reference.
:::

# Parameters

data

: Result differences to be analyzed.

sigma

: Values outside this number of sigma of std. dev.
  around mean are considered outliers.

# Returns

n_outliers

: Number of outlier data points.

modules.name: stsynphot.spectrum
documentation: This module contains spectrum classes specific to STScI formats.
Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum.reset_cache
documentation: Empty the reddening laws cache.

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum._interp_spec
documentation:
Interpolate between two spectra to parameter at given value.
Called by {func}`interpolate_spectral_element`.

# Parameters

interpval

: Desired parameter value.

waves

: Wavelengths for all the parameterized spectra.

lower_val, upper_val

: Low and high values of parameter to interpolate.

lower_thru, upper_thru

: Throughput values are `lower_val` and `upper_val`.

doshift

: Perform wavelength shift before interpolation.

# Returns

thru

: Interpolated throughput at `interpval`.

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum._extrap_spec
documentation:
Extrapolate using two spectra to parameter at given value.
Called by {func}`interpolate_spectral_element`.
Also see {func}`_interp_spec`.

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum.interpolate_spectral_element
documentation:
Interpolate (or extrapolate) throughput spectra in given
parameterized FITS table to given parameter value.

FITS table is parsed with {func}`stsynphot.stio.read_interp_spec`.
Parameterized values must be in ascending order in the
table columns.

If extrapolation is needed but not allowed, default throughput
from `THROUGHPUT` column will be used.

# Parameters

parfilename

: Parameterized filename contains a suffix followed by
  a column name specificationin between square brackets.
  For example, `path/acs_fr656n_006_syn.fits[fr656n#]`.

interpval

: Desired parameter value.

ext

: FITS extension index of the data table.

# Returns

sp

: Empirical bandpass at `interpval`.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.ExtrapolationNotAllowed

: Extrapolation is not allowed by data table.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: No columns available for interpolation or extrapolation.

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum.band
documentation:
Convenience function to create a bandpass with
an observation mode string.

See {func}`ObservationSpectralElement.from_obsmode` and
{ref}`stsynphot-obsmode`.

functions.name: stsynphot.spectrum.ebmvx
documentation:
Convenience function to create extinction curve for
given reddening law and $E(B-V)$.

# Parameters

redlaw_name

: Reddening law model name
  (see {meth}`synphot.reddening.ReddeningLaw.from_extinction_model`).
  Choose from 'lmc30dor', 'lmcavg', 'mwavg', 'mwdense', 'mwrv21',
  'mwrv40', 'smcbar', 'xgalsb', 'gal3', or `None`.
  `gal3` and `None` are same as `mwavg` and used for testing only.

ebv

: $E(B-V)$ value in magnitude. See
  {meth}`synphot.reddening.ReddeningLaw.extinction_curve`.

# Returns

extcurve

methods.name: stsynphot.spectrum.ObservationSpectralElement.thermback
documentation:
Calculate thermal background count rate for
`self.obsmode`.

Calculation uses
{func}`~stsynphot.observationmode.ObservationMode.thermal_spectrum`
to extract thermal component source spectrum in
PHOTLAM per square arcsec. Then this spectrum is
integrated and multiplied by detector pixel scale
and telescope collecting area to produce a count rate
in count/s/pix. This unit is non-standard but used widely
by STScI Exposure Time Calculator.

:::{note}
Similar to IRAF SYNPHOT THERMBACK.
:::

# Parameters

area

: Area that flux covers.
  If not a Quantity, assumed to be in $cm^{2}$.
  If `None`, use `area`.

thermtable

: Thermal component table filename.
  If `None`, uses `stsynphot.config.conf.thermtable`.

# Returns

bg

: Thermal background count rate.

# Raises

stsynphot.exceptions.PixscaleNotFoundError

: Undefined pixel scale for the given observation mode.

:::

Base class to handle an observation that uses the graph
and optical component tables, common to both optical and
thermal observation modes.

:::{note}
`modes` for parameterized mode is set such that the parameterized
value is stripped away; e.g., `'acs,wfc1,f555w,mjd#53000'`
becomes `['acs', 'wfc1', 'f555w', 'mjd#']`. Instead, the
parameterized value is kept in `pardict`; e.g., `'mjd#53000'`
becomes `{'mjd': 53000.0}`.

`pixscale` is set from `stsynphot.config.conf.detectorfile`,
which is parsed with {func}`stsynphot.stio.read_detector_pars`.

`binset` is set by `stsynphot.wavetable.WAVECAT`.
:::

# Parameters

obsmode

: Observation mode.

graphtable

: Graph table filename.
  If `None`, uses `stsynphot.config.conf.graphtable`.

comptable

: Optical component table filename.
  If `None`, uses `stsynphot.config.conf.comptable`.

# Attributes

modes

: List of individual modes within observation mode.

pardict

: Maps parameterized mode to its value.

gtname, ctname

: Graph and component table filenames.

compnames, thcompnames

: Optical and thermal components.

components

: List of component objects.

primary_area

: Telescope collecting area.

pixscale

: Detector pixel scale.

binset

: Wavelength table filename/param string from matching obsmode.

bandwave

: Wavelength set defined by `binset`.

modules.name: stsynphot.commissioning
documentation:
This sub-package is for commisioning tests.

:::{note}
Tests require ASTROLIB PYSYNPHOT to be installed.
Specifically, install the version to be compared against `stsynphot`.
:::

To run only the commissioning tests from source checkout:

```
cd ../..
pytest --pyargs stsynphot/commissioning --remote-data --slow
```

Like above, but also use `pytest-html` plugin for a detail HTML report:

```
pytest --pyargs stsynphot/commissioning --remote-data --slow --html=/full/path/report.html
```

To rerun only failed tests from the last run, add `--lf` in the list of
`args` above. This option requires `pytest-cache` (enabled by default)
and is useful for debugging.
Licensed under a 3-clause BSD style license - see LICENSE.rst

Class to handle `stsynphot.config.conf.wavecatfile` initialization
and access.

Input file is parsed with {func}`stsynphot.stio.read_wavecat`.

For each observation mode, its wavelength table is defined
by filename or parameter string. When it is accessed with
{py:meth}`~object.__getitem__`, the string is replaced by the actual
wavelengths array. If filename is given, it is parsed with
{func}`stsynphot.stio.read_waveset`.

# Parameters

fname

: Wavecat filename.

wave_unit

: Wavelength unit.

# Attributes

file

: Wavecat filename.

wave_unit

: Wavelength unit.

lookup

: Maps observation mode to corresponding wavelength table
  filename or parameter string.

setlookup

: Maps individual components of observation mode to its
  string. Used for partial matching.

documentation: GenericASTBuilder automagically constructs a concrete/abstract syntax tree
for a given input.  The extra argument is a class (not an instance!)
which supports the "__setslice__" and "__len__" methods.
XXX - silently overrides any user code in methods.

classes.name: stsynphot.spark.GenericASTTraversal
documentation: GenericASTTraversal is a Visitor pattern according to Design Patterns.  For
each node it attempts to invoke the method n_<node type>, falling
back onto the default() method if the n_* can't be found.  The preorder
traversal also looks for an exit hook named n_<node type>_exit (no default
routine is called if it's not found).  To prematurely halt traversal
of a subtree, call the prune() method -- this only makes sense for a
preorder traversal.  Node type is determined via the typestring() method.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.irafconvert
documentation:
Convert IRAF filename to regular filename.

Acceptable IRAF formats:

- `$path/file` - `path` assumed to be environment variable.
- `path$file` - `path` is special IRAF shortcut for TRDS
  data directory (case-insensitive).

Notes on special IRAF shortcut:

- `synphot` points to software data directory.

- `crrefer` points to `stsynphot.config.conf.rootdir`.

- Otherwise, decoded based on
  `stsynphot.config.conf.irafshortcutfile`
  that must contain the following named columns:

  > 1. `IRAFNAME` - The shortcut for look-up. If multiple matches
  >    are found, the first match is used.
  > 2. `RELPATH` - Path relative to
  >    `stsynphot.config.conf.rootdir`.

If separator is not found, input is returned as-is.

# Parameters

iraf_filename

: IRAF filename.

sep

: Path-file separator.

# Returns

reg_filename

: Regular filename.

# Raises

KeyError

: Environment variable or IRAF shortcut is undefined.

TypeError

: Input is not a string.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_graphtable
documentation:
Read graph table file.

Table must contain the following named columns:

1. `COMPNAME` - Component name, usually filter name (str)
2. `KEYWORD` - Usually instrument name (str)
3. `INNODE` - Input node number (int)
4. `OUTNODE`- Output node number (int)
5. `THCOMPNAME` - Thermal component name, usually filter name (str)
6. `COMMENT` - Comment (str)

Example:

| COMPNAME | KEYWORD | INNODE | OUTNODE | THCOMPNAME | COMMENT  |
| -------- | ------- | ------ | ------- | ---------- | -------- |
| clear    | nicmos  | 1      | 30      | clear      | idno=100 |
| clear    | wfc3    | 1      | 30      | clear      |          |
| clear    | wfpc    | 1      | 20      | clear      | idno=100 |

# Parameters

filename

: Graph table filename.
  If suffix is not 'fits' or 'fit', assume ASCII format.

tab_ext

: FITS extension index of the data table.
  This is ignored for ASCII file.

# Returns

primary_area

: Value of PRIMAREA keyword in primary header.
  Always `None` for ASCII file.

data

: Data table.

# Raises

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Failure to parse graph table.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_comptable
documentation:
Read component table file (regular or thermal).

Table must contain the following named columns:

1. `TIME` - Useafter date and time in the format of
   `MMM DD YYYY HH:MM:SS` (str)
2. `COMPNAME` - Component name, usually a combination of
   instrument/detector and filter names (str)
3. `FILENAME` - Path to corresponding throughput file in the
   format of `path_var$filename` (str)
4. `COMMENT` - Comment (str)

See {ref}`stsynphot-master-comp` for more details.

# Parameters

filename

: Component table filename.
  If suffix is not 'fits' or 'fit', assume ASCII format.

tab_ext

: FITS extension index of the data table.
  This is ignored for ASCII file.

# Returns

data

: Data table.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_catalog
documentation:
Read catalog grid look-up table.

Table must contain the following named columns:

1. `INDEX` - Grid values (str)
2. `FILENAME` - Relative file path and column name (str)

Example:

| INDEX          | FILENAME                    |
| -------------- | --------------------------- |
| 10000,-0.5,0.0 | ckm05/ckm05_10000.fits[g00] |
| 10000,-0.5,0.5 | ckm05/ckm05_10000.fits[g05] |
| 10000,-0.5,1.0 | ckm05/ckm05_10000.fits[g10] |

# Parameters

filename

: Catalog filename.
  If suffix is not 'fits' or 'fit', assume ASCII format.

tab_ext

: FITS extension index of the data table.
  This is ignored for ASCII file.

# Returns

data

: Data table.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_wavecat
documentation:
Read wavelength catalog from ASCII file.

Table must contain two columns without header.
Comment lines are allowed and will be ignored.
Columns are automatically named:

1. `OBSMODE` - Observation mode.
2. `FILENAME` - Corresponding wavelength table filename
   or parameters.

Example:

```
# WAVECAT.DAT -- Comments.
# More comments.
cos,fuv     (900.0,3000.0,1.0)
cos,nuv     (1000.0,12000.0,1.0)
acs,hrc     synphot$wavecats/acs.dat
acs,wfc1    synphot$wavecats/acs.dat
```

# Parameters

filename

: Wavelength catalog filename. Must be ASCII format.

# Returns

data

: Data table.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_waveset
documentation:
Read wavelength table from ASCII file.

Table must contain a single column without header.
Comment lines are allowed and will be ignored.
Column is automatically named `WAVELENGTH`.

Example:

```
# ACS.DAT -- Comments.
# More comments.
1000.
2000.
5000.
9000.
```

# Parameters

filename

: Wavelength table filename. Must be ASCII format.

wave_unit

: Wavelength unit.

# Returns

waveset

: Wavelength set array.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_detector_pars
documentation:
Read detector parameters from ASCII file.

Table must contain 4 columns without header.
Comment lines are allowed and will be ignored.
Columns are automatically named:

1. `OBSMODE` - Observation mode.
2. `SCALE` - Pixel scale in arcseconds.
3. `NX` - X dimension in pixels.
4. `NY` - Y dimension in pixels.

Example:

```
# DETECTORS.DAT -- Comments.
# More comments.
acs,hrc     0.027   1024  1024
acs,sbc     0.032   1024  1024
stis,g140l  0.0244  1024  1024
stis,g140m  0.0290  1024  1024
```

# Parameters

filename

: Detector parameters filename. Must be ASCII format.

# Returns

data

: Data table.

functions.name: stsynphot.stio.read_interp_spec
documentation:
Read parameterized (interpolate-able) throughput
spectra from FITS table.

Table must contain two or more columns:

1. `WAVELENGTH` - Wavelength values.

2. `PAR#VAL1` - First parameterized column.

3. `PAR#VAL2` - Second parameterized column.

4. ...

Example:

| WAVELENGTH | FR656N#6274.0 | FR656N#6331.4 |
| ---------- | ------------- | ------------- |
| 6136.0     | 0.0012254583  | 0.00019133692 |
| 6141.0     | 0.0016151578  | 0.00021833261 |
| 6146.0     | 0.0021577575  | 0.00025030275 |

# Parameters

filename

: FITS filename.

tab_ext

: FITS extension index of the data table.

# Returns

data

: Data table.

wave_unit

: Value of `TUNIT1` in table header.

do_wave_shift

: Perform wavelength shift before interpolation.
  This is `True` when primary header has `PARAMS`
  keyword set to `WAVELENGTH` (case-insensitive).

allow_extrap

: Allow extrapolation. This is only `True` when
  primary header has `EXTRAP` keyword explicitly
  set to `T` or `True`.

functions.name: stsynphot.catalog.grid_to_spec
documentation:
Extract spectrum from given catalog grid parameters.
Interpolate if necessary.

Grid parameters are read with {func}`get_catalog_index`.

# Parameters

gridname
: Model to use:
  : - `ck04models` - Castelli & Kurucz (2004)
    - `k93models` - Kurucz (1993)
    - `phoenix` - Allard et al. (2009)

t_eff

: Effective temperature of model.
  If not Quantity, assumed to be in Kelvin.
  If string (from parser), convert to Quantity.

metallicity

: Metallicity of model.
  If string (from parser), convert to float.

log_g

: Log surface gravity for model.
  If string (from parser), convert to float.

# Returns

sp

: Empirical source spectrum.

# Raises

stsynphot.exceptions.ParameterOutOfBounds

: Grid parameter out of bounds.

synphot.exceptions.SynphotError

: Invalid inputs.

functions.name: stsynphot.catalog.find_valid_g_phoenix
documentation:
Find valid `log_g` values in the Phoenix catalog.

:::{note}
Takes time to run because it has to find the gaps by
parsing the headers of individual data files in the catalog.
:::

# Returns

valid_g

: Dictionary mapping `(t_eff, metallicity)` to a list of valid
  `log_g` values.

uniq_metallicity

: Unique metallicity values in the catalog.
NOTE: The following functions need refactoring if you want to generalize
them to work on all the supported catalogs. This is because the catalogs
do not share the same header structure. Generalization requires reading all
the spectra tables, not just header, which will have a significant
performance impact.
Also see https://github.com/spacetelescope/stsynphot_refactor/issues/45

See {ref}`stsynphot-parser` for more details and
{func}`BaseParser.p_top` for language definition.

:::{note}
Like `stsynphot.spark`, parser docstrings in this module
are used by the parser itself, so modify any docstring in
this module with care.

In `Interpreter`, the docstring of every function named with `p_*`
is part of the instructions to the parser.

IRAF SYNPHOT extinction names are obsolete and no longer supported.
